economic_notes: OIB operates numerous business enterprises including a winery, golf course, campground/RV park, convenience store, forestry operations, ready-mix concrete, desert heritage centre, and development corporation. Lease revenues from Osoyoos Indian Band Holdings LLP totaled $1,784,748. OIB also guarantees housing loans for 13 members (~$4.0M) under First Nation Market Housing Fund.
notes: Audit was qualified because investments in business enterprises (equity method, ~$43.2M) were only subject to review engagement procedures, not audited. OIB has 3 consolidated subsidiaries and 11 GBEs/GBPs accounted for by modified equity method. Prior year (2019) restated to reallocate $2,723,589 from cash to restricted cash and from unrestricted to internally restricted surplus. education_expenses includes both Education ($1,066,608) and Sen*Pok*Chin Education ($1,748,950). OSR calculated as total revenue minus government transfers (DISC + BC + OTD + FNHA + Federal + Grants + CMHC + ONA = $11,530,255); osr = $20,778,755 - $11,530,255 = $9,248,500. Annual debt service reflects repayment of long-term debt per cash flow statement.
